name: Bank of Commerce
description_short: FDIC-insured bank in Rawlins offering free checking and savings accounts with rewards through Kasasa, plus home mortgage services.
description_long: Bank of Commerce is a locally-owned bank based in Rawlins that operates as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank positions itself as a community-focused alternative to larger national banks, emphasizing personalized service and local decision-making. The institution offers a focused product suite centered on deposit accounts and residential mortgages. Their primary offerings include Kasasa® Free Rewards Checking accounts (advertising up to 4% APY), Kasasa® Free Rewards Savings accounts, online banking with login and enrollment capabilities, and home mortgage lending for property purchases. All deposit accounts are FDIC-insured, providing standard federal protections up to applicable limits. Bank of Commerce distinguishes itself through the Kasasa rewards program integration, which rewards customers for meeting simple qualification criteria (described as "so easy, you're probably already doing it"). The bank emphasizes ease of switching from competitors and markets itself as a "home-owned bank," suggesting local or community ownership structure. Their website focuses on accessibility and simplified account opening. The bank's profile is limited by what appears to be a relatively narrow service menu compared to full-service regional banks—the website does not advertise business lending, investment services, credit cards, or other extended financial products. As a smaller community bank, availability may be geographically restricted, and product features/rates are not transparently listed on the homepage, requiring customers to contact the bank directly or visit in person for detailed information.
services: Kasasa Free Rewards Checking with APY incentives
Password recovery and account access assistance
Callback request service for customer service inquiries
Account switching assistance for new customers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with federal government backing
Kasasa rewards checking offering up to 4% APY on deposits
Free checking and savings accounts with no mentioned monthly fees
Simple qualification criteria for rewards accounts (no complex requirements listed)
Online banking platform with account login and enrollment
Home mortgage lending to support property purchases
Local/home-owned bank structure emphasizing community relationship
Easy account switching process highlighted for new customers
cons: Limited geographic availability (Rawlins location suggests regional-only service)
Narrow product range with no advertised business accounts, credit cards, or investment services
APY rates and account terms not transparently posted—requires contacting bank directly
Minimal website content limits ability to compare features and pricing before engagement
No mention of mobile app, mobile banking, or advanced digital services
best_for: Local Rawlins/Wyoming residents seeking community banking with FDIC protection
Consumers wanting rewards checking without complex qualification requirements
First-time homebuyers or refinancing customers in the bank's service area
People prioritizing local/home-owned bank relationships over national chain alternatives
diagnosis: Bank of Commerce is best for Wyoming residents (particularly Rawlins area) seeking straightforward FDIC-insured deposit accounts with rewards, or homebuyers needing local mortgage lending. The main caveat is that geographic availability appears limited to their Rawlins location, and the shallow product range and opaque pricing require direct contact to evaluate competitiveness against regional and national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
